items.title: Trump is threatening a new trade war . The EU should call his bluff
Gold Declines as Fresh US-Iran Tension Fans Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Gold declined to near $4,000 an ounce after the US and Iran traded attacks in the Persian Gulf, straining a ceasefire that had last week seen energy prices fall to pre-war levels and help temper expectations for an interest-rate hike.

items.title: PBOC’s New Overnight Rate Said Below Forecasts in Hint at Easing
items.summary: China’s central bank set the interest rate on its new overnight liquidity tool at a level that was below expectations, according to people familiar with the matter, in what some economists see as a de facto rate cut that could push down market borrowing costs.

With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: AI Demand , War , & Climate Pressure Push World Back To Nuclear
Philippines becomes world’s top solar spender amid Middle East energy crisis
items.summary:
People in the Philippines are flocking to install solar power on rooftops and escape the burden of soaring electricity prices, making it the world’s biggest spender on solar panels since the war in Iran started.

items.summary: Rates and currency volatility could dominate the second half of the year.

items.title: Russia Expands LNG Dark Fleet Effort With a 19-Year-Old Tanker
items.summary: A liquefied natural gas tanker docked at a US-sanctioned storage unit in Russia’s Murmansk region, the first time the vessel has loaded blacklisted fuel and the latest sign of Moscow’s efforts to expand exports despite Western sanctions.

items.title: Copper Declines as Fed Stance and Strong Dollar Weigh On Metals
items.summary: Copper fell as the Federal Reserve’s hawkish stance and a rising US dollar continued to weigh on sentiment toward industrial metals.

items.title: Reserve Bank interest rates : RBA could use more aggressive rate cuts in next economic crisis
US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
items.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: China widens Japan export curbs, targeting drone makers, nuclear firms and defense institutes
items.summary: China blacklisted four of Japan's government defense research institutes and placed dozens more Japanese firms under tightened export restrictions Monday.

items.title: Japan’s new AI police chief takes on US$2 billion scam epidemic
items.summary:
She has a young woman’s voice, a friendly face and a police chief’s badge. Her name is “AIko” and she may be Japan’s most unconventional weapon in a war against fraud that cost the country a record US$2 billion last year.
AIko – a blend of the abbreviation for artificial intelligence and “ko”, the Japanese feminine name suffix – made her public debut in late May on Osaka Prefectural Police’s YouTu

EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

Mideast Live Updates: U.S. Indicates a Deal to Suspend Attacks in Strait but Iran Hasn’t Confirmed
items.summary: A U.S. official said the U.S. and Iran agreed to halt attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and allow vessels to move freely. Hostilities in recent days have threatened a two-week-old cease-fire.

items.title: Australian energy exploration hits 10-year high as Asian gas demand surges
items.summary:
Energy exploration has picked up sharply in Australia, driven ⁠by growing Asian gas demand, technological advances and an improved investment climate, with the Iran war ⁠underscoring the urgency to develop supply after years of sluggish spending.

‘Pass on the warmth’: blind Chinese teen aces gaokao, chooses medicine to help others
items.summary:
A teenager from northern China who lost his sight in a brutal attack has emerged as one of the top scorers in the national college entrance examination for students with disabilities.
Thirteen years ago, Guo Bin was just six years old when a middle-aged woman lured him away from his home in Shanxi province.
After threatening to gouge out his eyes if he continued to cry, the woman attacked him with

items.title: Oil prices rise as US, Iranian strikes threaten Strait of Hormuz reopening
items.summary: Brent crude edges up as tit-for-tat strikes imperial return to normality in key waterway.

U.S., Iran pause hostilities as Hormuz shipping resumes after weekend clashes
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran agreed to pause hostilities and allow commercial vessels to move freely through the Strait of Hormuz, easing fears of a prolonged disruption to global oil supplies after a weekend of military exchanges.
